DISCOVERY 610, PLAINTIFF FRANK CARON'S MOTION TO COMPEL FURTHER RESPONSES TO REQUEST FOR PRODUCTION OF DOCUMENTS (SET #1 AND #2) IS GRANTED IN PART. THE MOTION IS WITHDRAWN AS TO REQUEST #11. THE PRELIMINARY STATEMENT AND GENERAL OBJECTIONS ARE STRICKEN. THERE IS NO PROVISION IN THE CODE OF CIVIL PROCEDURE FOR GENERAL OBJECTIONS. AS TO REQUESTS 6, 12-14, 15-18, 20-22, THE OBJECTIONS OTHER THAN BASED ON THE ATTORNEY CLIENT OR ATTORNEY WORK PRODUCT PRIVILEGES ARE OVERRULED AND STRICKEN. DEFENDANT SHALL PRODUCE LOCOMOTIVE RECORDS FOR 6 MONTHS PRIOR AND 1 MONTH SUBSEQUENT TO THE INCIDENTS FOR THE RESPECTIVE TRAINS. ANY DOCUMENTS WITHHELD SHALL BE IDENTIFIED WITH PARTICULARITY IN A PRIVILEGE LOG. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. NOT REPORTED.

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Production Of Documents And Seperate Statement Of Discovery Items
Off Calendar Mar-21-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: There were no appearances. Mr. Richard E. Crow, II (who previously requested a telephonic appearance) submitted on the Court's tentative ruling. Tentative ruling was adopted as follows: Plaintiff Frank Caron's motion to compel further responses to request for production of documents (set #1 and #2) is granted in part. The motion is withdrawn as to request #11. The preliminary statement and general objections are stricken. There is no provision in the Code of Civil Procedure for general objections. As to requests 6, 12-14, 15-18, 20-22, the objections other than based on the attorney client or attorney work product privileges are overruled and stricken. Defendant shall produce locomotive records for 6 months prior and 1 month subsequent to the incidents for the respective trains. Any documents withheld shall be identified with particularity in a privilege log. COMMISSIONER: BRUCE E. CHAN. Not reported. (610/BEC)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Dec-18-2007 Set For Trial On Jun-23-08 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Jun-05-08 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 611
